join_order.notes: sub_valid bitset[86135] = 10.5 KB L1-resident; sub_sic_arr and sub_cik_arr flat arrays for O(1) lookup by adsh_code

Scan pre sequentially: for each row check stmt==eq_code, then adsh_code in sub_valid; insert (adsh,tag,ver) packed uint64_t key into flat open-addressing hash set; 64K entries x 8 bytes = 512 KB fits in L2. Single sequential pre scan replaces random binary searches into 154 MB pre_key_sorted.

join_order.probe: result groups only (deferred to output decode)
join_order.notes: Defer tag join entirely to output decode stage. Sampling confirmed tag abstract=0 filter eliminates 0 rows from qualifying set. During scan, skip all tag lookups. After aggregation, for each unique (tag_code, ver_code) in result groups, compute FNV combined hash, probe tag_pk_hash (67 MB, few K probes = negligible), get row_id, check abstract[row_id], read tlabel. This eliminates the 1175ms build_tag_join_map phase entirely.

filters.method: zone map block skip (uom_min>0 means no USD); then per-row uom_code==0 check
std::isnan(v) == false; empirically no NULLs among USD rows
sub_valid bitset probe on num.adsh_code; 10.5 KB L1-resident
pre_eq_sub_set flat hash probe; O(1) per num row; 512 KB L2-resident; replaces O(log 9.6M) binary search on 154 MB pre_key_sorted
Deferred to output: for each result group's (tag_code, ver_code), probe tag_pk_hash -> check abstract[row_id]; sampling confirmed 0 rows eliminated during scan

data_structures.sub_valid_bitset: uint64_t[(86135+63)/64] = 10.5 KB; marks adsh_codes where sic in [4000,4999]; L1-resident
data_structures.sub_sic_array: int16_t[86135] = 168 KB; direct lookup sic value by adsh_code
data_structures.sub_cik_array: int32_t[86135] = 336 KB; direct lookup cik by adsh_code
data_structures.pre_eq_sub_set: flat open-addressing hash set; key=uint64_t packed (adsh_code<<34 | tag_code<<17 | ver_code); capacity=next_pow2(64656*2)=131072; 131072*8=1 MB; L2-resident; empty sentinel=UINT64_MAX; probe cost O(1) average; replaces 154 MB pre_key_sorted binary search
data_structures.tag_fnv_arrays: uint64_t h_tag[198312] (1.5 MB) + uint64_t h_ver[83816] (0.65 MB); precomputed FNV64a hashes of dict strings; used only at output decode stage for tag_pk_hash probing
data_structures.thread_local_agg: per-thread flat_hash_map<uint64_t, pair<double,int64_t>> keyed by packed(sic<<48 | (uint32_t)tag_code<<17 | (uint32_t)ver_code); reserved to 8192 entries; num qualifying rows ~530K / 64 threads = ~8K rows per thread
data_structures.thread_local_cik: per-thread vector<CIKEntry{uint64_t group_key, int32_t cik}> for COUNT DISTINCT; sorted globally post-scan

parallelism.pre_scan: parallel over pre blocks (9600799 rows / 96 blocks of 100K); thread-local sets unioned into pre_eq_sub_set; alternatively single-threaded since build is 18ms
parallelism.num_scan: morsel-driven over num blocks (395 blocks); atomic block counter; each thread processes complete blocks
parallelism.merge_phase: sort-merge: collect all thread-local (group_key, sum, count) tuples into flat array, sort by group_key, linear scan to aggregate; CIK dedup via global sort of CIK entries

indexes.purpose: block-level USD filter: skip blocks where uom_min > 0 (no USD rows); uom=0 so blocks with uom_max < 0 are entirely non-USD

output decode only: for each unique (tag_code, ver_code) in result groups, compute combined FNV hash, probe tag_pk_hash to get tag row_id, check abstract[row_id], read tlabel; only few K probes post-aggregation

aggregation.key: uint64_t packed(int16_t sic << 48 | uint32_t tag_code << 17 | uint32_t ver_code) — 8 bytes; tag_code < 198312 < 2^18; ver_code < 83816 < 2^17
aggregation.accumulator: double sum_value, int64_t cnt_value (for AVG = sum/cnt); CIK collected in separate vector for COUNT DISTINCT
aggregation.post_scan_tlabel_merge: after resolve_tlabels phase, groups with same (sic, tlabel_string) are merged into final result; handles case of multiple (tag_code, ver_code) sharing same tlabel

columns: t.tlabel (decoded via tag_pk_hash lookup at output stage)
COUNT(DISTINCT s.cik) = distinct cik count from sorted CIK array

optimization_notes: Root cause: build_tag_join_map (1175ms, 83% of total) iterates 1.07M tag rows with string-keyed hash map operations — 3.2M std::string allocations + 2M string-hash lookups + 1M tlabel copies. Sampling revealed two key facts: (1) tag_pk_hash is 67 MB (exceeds 44 MB LLC), so random probing during scan is cache-hostile; (2) a compact pre_eq_sub_set of 64K entries (1 MB, L2-resident) fully replaces the 154 MB pre_key_sorted binary search. Strategy: Category A (plan restructuring) — eliminate the tag table iteration entirely. Pre-side scan (pre/stmt.bin + pre/adsh.bin + pre/tag.bin + pre/version.bin) with EQ + sub_valid filter builds a 64K-key flat hash set in ~18ms (vs 1175ms for tag map). During num scan, probe this tiny pre_eq_sub_set instead of binary-searching pre_key_sorted (2× faster scan). The tag abstract check and tlabel resolution are deferred to output decode stage (few K group probes — negligible). This reduces total estimated time from 1410ms to ~80-100ms by eliminating the dominant 1175ms build phase and cutting scan overhead.
